sanskrit: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् । बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पृथानन्दन  सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका अनादि बीज मुझे जान ।  बुद्धिमानोंमें बुद्धि और तेजस्वियोंमें तेज मैं हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Know Me, O Partha, as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the brilliance of the brilliant.
essence: All brilliance is borrowed from the same source.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he is the original source of every living being, and that intelligence and brilliance in people come from him.
simpleInsight: You can stop treating brilliance as personal ownership and start seeing it as something received.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is frozen before the war. Krishna shifts the vision from fear to origin: every being comes from him, and every clear mind and radiant strength is his presence shining through.
modernRelevance: You watch someone speak with rare clarity in a tense room and feel small beside them. The verse says that clarity itself is not privately owned; it flows from a deeper source.
translationLiteral: बीजम् (seed) / माम् (Me) / सर्वभूतानाम् (of all beings) / विद्धि (know) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सनातनम् (eternal) / बुद्धिः (intelligence) / बुद्धिमताम् (of the intelligent) / अस्मि (I am) / तेजः (brilliance) / तेजस्विनाम् (of the brilliant) / अहम् (I am)
insight: It shifts attention from personal pride to the shared source behind intelligence, vitality, and presence.

quoteVariants.text: Know the divine as the eternal seed in all beings. Intelligence and brilliance rise from that source.
One source. All intelligence. All brilliance.
What shines in minds and beings comes from one source.
See one source in all brilliance.
